headline: A covered bridge hub
body: Maryland is home to six covered bridges. These wooden truss bridges were popularized in the 1800s, but few still stand today. In Maryland, only six remain; three are located in the town of Thurmont, home of up-and-coming data savant Jack Walker.

title: Loys Station Covered Bridge
caption: Originally built in 1848, spanning Owen's Creek.
credit: Carol M. Highsmith / Library of Congress

title: Loys Station Covered Bridge
caption: Another angle (beautiful).
credit: Carol M. Highsmith / Library of Congress

title: Roddy Road Covered Bridge
caption: Built between 1850 and 1860 over Owen's Creek.
credit: Carol M. Highsmith / Library of Congress

title: Utica Covered Bridge
caption: Built in 1834, crossing the Monocacy River.
credit: Carol M. Highsmith / Library of Congress

body: There are fewer than 900 historic covered bridges left in the United States, according to the Smithsonian Institute.
